summary.intent: Clean up the legacy Session table and hook handler code, fix git username display in search results to use per-commit authors, fix the checkpoint polling loop to pick up remote changes, and add scroll-to and highlight behavior when navigating from search results to session details.
summary.outcome: Hook handler and SDK CLI entry points were deleted entirely; Session/Event/AgentParent/PendingToolUse and all old v1 checkpoint tables were removed from the schema; git usernames now source from CheckpointMetadata populated at index time from commit authors; the polling loop correctly resets to origin branch after fetch; search results navigate to session detail with scroll-to-event and yellow highlight animation; thinking blocks are now rendered as collapsible sections in ClaudeTurnCard.

repo: FTS5 virtual tables (LogContentFts and its shadow tables) must be dropped manually via sqlite3 CLI before `prisma db push` — Prisma cannot handle them and will error out every time the schema changes.

The gossamer server reads gitUser from local git config at startup, but the correct source for per-checkpoint authors is CheckpointMetadata.gitUserName/gitUserEmail populated by saveGitOidMapping via findCommitForCheckpoint.
logEventsToEvents converts LogEventItem[] to synthetic Event[] with negative IDs; the real LogEvent.id must be threaded through as _sourceLogEventId/_extraSourceLogEventIds for DOM targeting.
Thinking content blocks from Claude are present in LogEvent data (and indexed by FTS) but were never rendered in the UI — they are now shown as collapsible sections in ClaudeTurnCard.
The checkpoint polling loop was fetching remote commits but resetting to the local branch tip, so remote changes from teammates were never applied to the worktree.
Mantine ScrollArea renders its own internal scrollable viewport div — window.scrollIntoView() has no effect; must use viewportRef to manually calculate scroll position.
The gossamer serve entry point is src/serve.ts, bin is gossamer-serve → dist/serve.js; run from /Users/stephanostsoucas/gossamer with `npm run serve -- --repo-dir <path>`.

code.finding: Checkpoint polling reset was using local branch ref instead of origin/entire/checkpoints/v1 after fetch, causing remote commits to be fetched but never applied. Fixed to reset --hard origin/${CHECKPOINT_BRANCH} with fallback to local branch if remote unreachable.
findCommitForCheckpoint now returns authorName and authorEmail from gitlog fields, used by saveGitOidMapping to populate CheckpointMetadata.
saveGitOidMapping now calls db.checkpointMetadata.updateMany to write gitUserName/gitUserEmail from the linked commit author onto CheckpointMetadata.

logEventsToEvents tracks currentTurnLogEventIds accumulating all assistant event IDs per turn (including thinking-only events) so Stop events carry _extraSourceLogEventIds for correct scroll targeting. Thinking text is collected into thinkingBlocks array and passed through Stop event data to ClaudeTurnCard.

When item isTarget, data-log-event-id is set to targetLogEventId (not sourceIds[0]) to ensure querySelector finds the element — sourceIds[0] for a claudeTurn is a tool-use ID, not the assistant message ID.

workflow: When killing a Node server process, pkill -f pattern matching is unreliable — use lsof -i :<port> -P | grep LISTEN to find the exact PID and kill it directly.
After changing directories for a sub-project command (e.g., cd app/ for tsc), remember to return to the repo root before running server commands.
Adding diagnostic console.log statements to print query params, data-attribute values on DOM elements, and the IDs being queried is the fastest way to debug scroll/targeting mismatches.

friction: FTS5 virtual tables blocked prisma db push twice — required manual sqlite3 DROP TABLE commands each time the schema changed.
Old handler.js process kept running after pkill attempts, serving stale code and causing confusion about whether fixes were working.
Server was accidentally started from the wrong directory (app/) after a type-check, causing 'Missing script' errors.
gitUserName returned null in search results through two iterations of fixes before the correct approach (sourcing from CheckpointMetadata) was reached.
Scroll did not work for three consecutive attempts: first Mantine ScrollArea viewport issue, then wrong sourceId for claudeTurn items, then thinking blocks never included in sourceIds.
open_items: Live DB still contains old tables (Event, Session, AgentParent, PendingToolUse, Checkpoint, CheckpointSession, etc.) that were removed from the schema — they will be dropped on next `prisma db push` with --accept-data-loss.
Thinking sections in ClaudeTurnCard are rendered collapsed by default — could auto-expand the thinking section when it is the scroll target from a search result.

GitUserName/gitUserEmail on CheckpointMetadata is only populated when saveGitOidMapping runs (i.e., when a checkpoint is linked to a commit) — shadow sessions or unlinked checkpoints fall back to local git config, which is wrong for multi-user repos.
Diagnostic console.log statements added for scroll debugging were not removed before the session ended.
